threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it the 'Overground' when everyone knows it's just the 'G' line here. You'll get used to the lingo, eventually.
Caught a glimpse of two guys in suits deep in a debate about the best kebab joints at 3 AM. Brixton’s late-night life never sleeps.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw a guy in a suit next to a street artist spraying graffiti, both laughing at the same joke. That's Neukölln for you.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain will you find a kiosk where you can grab a beer and a döner at 2 AM, all while standing next to a guy playing the accordion.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: Locals know to carry an extra pair of socks after a night out; those potholes can suddenly turn into puddles when it rains.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 21 at Civic Center yesterday, and just as we rolled out, a guy in a full clown suit hopped on. No one batted an eye.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think the loudest bars are the most fun, but the best moments are often in the quiet plazas where locals share a simple beer and laughter.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They still scan menus for English options at local tapas bars, missing the joy of ordering a surprise dish from the waiter.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call the Overground the 'train' and not the 'tube'—that's a sure giveaway you're new around these parts.
Caught a night bus from Brixton last weekend. A guy in a lion onesie was serenading the driver with Disney tunes. Only in London, right?

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The first time I saw a street artist painting a mural on the wall of an old Turkish market, I knew I was home. It's that vibe.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg will you find a spontaneous techno party in a park on a Wednesday afternoon—complete with a mobile bakery serving fresh bread.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: Only a true local knows to avoid the Civic Center BART entrance when there’s smoke; the tourists just freeze and take selfies.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 5-Fulton today. A guy in a tuxedo was on his way to a wedding but got stuck for 20 minutes because of a dog on the tracks. Classic San Francisco.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think the metro is the best way to explore, but they miss the nooks and crannies of Gràcia that you can only find on foot.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell they’re new by their hesitation at the metro turnstiles, fumbling with T10 cards instead of swiping like a local.
